questions.question:
Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently:
A. annoyed
B. balloon
C. passport
D. abroad
questions.explanation: Balloon has the /u:/ sound whereas the others contain the /ɔ/ sound.

questions.question:
Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently:
A. buses
B. bicycles
C. clothes
D. rules
questions.explanation: Buses ends with the /ɪz/ sound.

questions.question:
Choose the word that has a different stressed syllable from the rest:
A. suitcase
B. although
C. ticket
D. subway
questions.explanation: Although has primary stress on the second syllable.

questions.question:
Choose the word that has a different stressed syllable from the rest:
A. decorate
B. educate
C. uniform
D. discover
questions.explanation: Discover has primary stress on the second syllable.

questions.question: I never take the bus because buses are __________ than any other transport in my town.
questions.explanation: Slower is the comparative form of slow.

questions.question: When I go hiking, I often take some food and water in my ________.
questions.explanation: A backpack is typically used for carrying food and water when hiking.

questions.question: The teacher was __________ because some students were talking in class and weren’t paying attention to the lesson.
questions.explanation: Teachers get annoyed when students don't pay attention.

questions.question: I’m not going out tonight; I have a lot of math __________ to do.
questions.explanation: At home, students typically do homework.

questions.question:
Student A: Where’s Andy? Can you see him?
Student B: Over there! He’s the boy with a _________ bag.
questions.explanation: Adjective order: Size -> Age -> Color.

questions.question: Peter _________ likes his school because he has a lot of friends there and the teachers don’t give him too much homework.
questions.explanation: 'Really' is used here to emphasize the verb likes.

questions.question:
Person A: Excuse me, is this suitcase _______?
Person B: No, it’s not _________.
questions.explanation: Both need to be possessive pronouns.

questions.question: I think people should cycle more. It’s more __________ because it doesn’t cause air pollution.
questions.explanation: Not causing air pollution makes cycling eco-friendly.

questions.question: He lost his __________, so he had to contact the embassy. He needs it to fly back to his home country.
questions.explanation: You contact the embassy when you lose your passport in a foreign country.

questions.question:
Student A: Let’s go bowling tonight. There’s a new bowling alley near my house.
Student B: _________. I have to finish my book report.
questions.explanation: Because they have homework, they must politely decline.

questions.question: What does that sign mean?
options: Be careful! People are watching your step.
You have to look at the way you step.
You should be careful when you are walking.
You have to look at your feet when you are walking.
questions.correctAnswer: You should be careful when you are walking.
questions.explanation: This signifies being cautious while walking.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
options: You must drive faster than 15km/h in the school zone.
Students should be at school from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m.
The speed limit is not for weekends.
Students shouldn’t drive on school days.
questions.correctAnswer: The speed limit is not for weekends.
questions.explanation: The sign indicates time restrictions, commonly excluding weekends.

questions.question: Timothy was __________ at the result of his geography test. (DISAPPOINT)
Ben came top of the class in all subjects and his parents were so __________. (SURPRISE)
Traveling around the UK by train is a fast and __________ way to explore the country. (COMFORT)
Although I prepared carefully, I got nervous when giving my __________. (PRESENT)
Studying abroad makes you more __________ and open to new experiences. (DEPEND)
During the Lazarim Carnival in Portugal, there are singing __________. (COMPETE)

questions.transcript: Ms. Johnson: Jimmy, I enjoyed your book report on Wanderlove...
questions.question: The teacher, Ms. Johnson, enjoyed Jimmy’s (23)____ on a story about a young girl...

questions.transcript: Teacher: The story reminded me of the time I got to study abroad in Brazil.
questions.question: The story reminded the teacher about when she got to (24)____ in Brazil.

questions.transcript: Teacher: ...you will need to pass a test to get accepted to the school.
questions.question: The teacher told Jimmy he would need to (25)____ a test to get accepted to the school.

questions.transcript: Jimmy: That sounds great! My parents will be so delighted to hear this news.
questions.question: Jimmy thought his parents would be so (26)____ to hear about this news.

questions.transcript: Teacher: ...remember you will need to study hard and complete all your homework...
questions.question: The teacher also told Jimmy to study (27)____ and finish all his homework to get good grades.
What is this vlog about?
questions.explanation: The vlog primarily talks about Japan's maglev train, a new public transportation.

questions.question: What type of transportation is the maglev?
questions.explanation: It's the new maglev train.

questions.question: Why is the maglev comfortable to ride?
options: It feels like riding on air.
questions.correctAnswer: It feels like riding on air.
questions.explanation: David states: 'It feels like riding on air.'

questions.question: How fast can the maglev train go?
questions.explanation: It goes 400 kph FASTER than the 100 kph trains. So 500 kph.

questions.question: What makes the maglev reliable?
options: It feels like riding on air.
questions.explanation: David states the train was always on time.

questions.passage: This story is about how my (33)______ got lost on vacation. Last summer, I took an airplane to the Bahamas. I heard that airports are not always reliable and sometimes lose luggage. So, I put all my clothes in a (34)______ suitcase that would stand out from the others, and I put my phone and camera in a small backpack to take on the plane. I checked in my suitcase using my boarding pass and got on the plane. When the plane landed, I went to the (35)______ area to pick it up. I waited and waited, but it never came. I went to customs and showed them my (36)______ and asked them about my suitcase, but they had not seen it. I looked everywhere. Luckily, I still had my (37)______ with my phone in it. I was able to use it to call the airline and find my missing suitcase.
questions.question: (33) A. boarding pass   B. luggage   C. backpack   D. phone

questions.passage:
Journal Entry 15/07/2020 by Jennifer
I just returned home from a year of studying abroad in America. It's only been a few days, and I am already missing my new friends. I will have to keep in touch and follow them on social media because they were fun to play with and helped me in school. Sometimes, they would see that I would get really annoyed when I failed my tests, so they started helping me with my homework. I was lucky to have such great friends. Studying abroad was good because it showed me how hard work pays off, although it wasn't always easy. One time, I remember how disappointed my mom was when I told her I did not pass my science test. But, after lots of practice, my English improved, and I started to do better on my tests, which pleased her.
questions.question: Jennifer started missing her new friends a year after returning home. (T/F/NI)
questions.explanation: It's only been a few days, not a year.

questions.question: Rewrite using DIFFICULT: The English exam wasn’t as easy as I thought.
correctAnswers: The English exam was more difficult than I thought.

questions.question: Rewrite using Although: Ivy studied a lot for her biology test. However, she didn’t pass it.
correctAnswers: Although Ivy studied a lot for her biology test, she didn't pass it.

questions.question:
Rewrite: It’s the rules to remove your shoes and belts when you go through the airport security checkpoint.
You have...
correctAnswers: You have to remove your shoes and belts when you go through the airport security checkpoint.

questions.question:
Rewrite: It’s not necessary for Laura to take part in the sports day if she doesn’t want to.
Laura doesn't...
correctAnswers: Laura doesn't have to take part in the sports day if she doesn't want to.
Laura doesn't need to take part in the sports day if she doesn't want to.

questions.question: Make a sentence: Jack / pleased / because / he / get / A / biology test.
correctAnswers: Jack was pleased because he got an A on the biology test.
Jack is pleased because he got an A on the biology test.

questions.question: Make a sentence: Trains / be / most popular form / public transport / the United Kingdom.
correctAnswers: Trains are the most popular form of public transport in the United Kingdom.

questions.question: Make a sentence: Riding / bike / fast / go / bus / in rush hours.
correctAnswers: Riding a bike is faster than going by bus in rush hours.

questions.question: Make a sentence: Tommy / not have / as much / homework / his sister.
correctAnswers: Tommy doesn't have as much homework as his sister.
Tommy doesn't have as much homework as his sister does.
